lifecycle_rows.label: Upload staging
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Keep current versions only until scan, validation, request binding, and attachment confirmation settle or replacement supersedes the session.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Preserve prior versions and upload-part lineage until the upload session is superseded or recovery law closes the window.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: No public hold path; operational holds remain gateway-controlled and audit-bound.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: No cold-tier transition. Staging remains hot-access until it is promoted, quarantined, or retired.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Derived assets must not outlive their upload-session and evidence binding without explicit limitation notes.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 1. Reference runtime topology
source_refs.rationale: The runtime topology explicitly requires an immutable object store for uploaded bytes, quarantined binaries, proof bundles, and export bodies.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Decision
source_refs.rationale: ADR-002 already fixed the object-store role as immutable-body storage distinct from control truth, broker transport, and disposable projections.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: OBJECT_STORAGE_AND_QUARANTINE_BUCKETS dependency row
source_refs.rationale: The dependency register marks the object-store substrate as mandatory while leaving the platform as a procurement or platform choice.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: EvidenceItem and DOCUMENTARY_EVIDENCE candidate-boundary law
source_refs.rationale: Object storage carries retained evidence bodies but must not turn raw bytes or extracted output into canonical truth by itself.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Secure document-upload flow and preview posture
source_refs.rationale: Transfer, scan, validation, attachment confirmation, preview, and current-vs-history remain separate facts and the storage topology must preserve that separation.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2.2 Customer/Client portal and upload-session surfaces
source_refs.rationale: Upload-session allocation freezes tenant, client, request, and request-version identity, which object keys and refs must preserve.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Upload-session identity and stale-rebase law
source_refs.rationale: Storage keys and refs must continue to bind immutable upload-session identity through rebase and replacement instead of collapsing onto friendly names.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Frozen upload-session continuity and scanner-delay cases
source_refs.rationale: Multipart or resumable transfer success does not imply accepted, previewable, or attachable posture, and storage event routes must preserve that law.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Artifact retention contract and limitation behavior
source_refs.rationale: Lifecycle rules must bind storage purposes to retention classes and explicit limitation behavior rather than inventing ad hoc expiry semantics.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Artifact retention matrix explicit gaps and retention classes
source_refs.rationale: The corpus publishes retention classes but still lacks one canonical per-artifact duration matrix, so lifecycle rules must encode the gap explicitly.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Truth boundary statement and quarantine posture
source_refs.rationale: The object-store topology has to preserve the already-fixed quarantine, preview, and extraction boundary from upload intake safety work.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Release rows and history-preserving copy/promote semantics
source_refs.rationale: Quarantine release is evidence-bound and must preserve history rather than mutating a quarantined object into clean state in place.

notes: Staging bytes are mutable only through new object-version refs; direct overwrite does not redefine the authoritative upload ref.

lifecycle_rows.label: Retained evidence
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Current retained evidence versions remain available for gateway-governed preview or download while the evidence window is lawful.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Prior versions remain recoverable and queryable for lineage, replay, and accidental overwrite recovery.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Legal hold or statutory block prevents lifecycle delete and requires durable limitation or hold notes.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: Cold or archive transitions are provider-allowed only when retrieval remains compatible with replay, enquiry, and restore requirements.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: If source evidence expires, surviving previews or exports must degrade into explicit limitation or placeholder posture rather than appearing fully evidentiary.

lifecycle_rows.label: Authority payload bodies
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Authority payload bodies persist as immutable evidence and ingress lineage anchors.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Superseded or retried payload versions remain queryable by immutable ref and never collapse onto one mutable blob.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Legal hold and investigation posture override automatic deletion.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: Archive transitions are allowed only when restore and authority investigation windows remain satisfied.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Any surviving normalized or projected views must point back to limitation or omission notes once payload bodies expire.

lifecycle_rows.label: Derived previews
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Preview derivatives remain cache-like but still versioned so the gateway can preserve current-versus-history posture explicitly.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Older preview versions may be trimmed earlier than retained evidence so long as current-versus-history labels and omission notes remain accurate.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Investigative holds preserve preview lineage only when a separate evidence anchor still exists.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: No archive tier by default; previews are expected to remain fast-access or be deleted once limited or superseded.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: When the underlying evidence expires, previews survive only with limitation notes or placeholder posture.

lifecycle_rows.label: Masked exports
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Masked exports remain current only for the request, route, masking, and delivery-binding window that created them.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Superseded masked exports remain independently addressable until the export evidence window closes.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Hold prevents deletion but does not widen the allowed audience for the export.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: Cold transition allowed only after delivery and replay needs are closed.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Masked exports that outlive source evidence must surface retention-limited posture explicitly.

lifecycle_rows.label: Restricted exports
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Restricted exports remain step-up-bound, operator-governed artifacts rather than customer-safe downloads.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Prior restricted exports remain versioned for audit and investigation but never become default delivery targets.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Hold preserves the artifact while keeping access narrow and auditable.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: Archive allowed only when operator-access expectations and replay windows still remain satisfiable.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Restricted exports must preserve limitation notes if the underlying evidence expires first.

notes: The masking variant is a first-class lifecycle dimension, not an annotation on one shared export blob.

lifecycle_rows.label: Quarantine
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Quarantined current versions remain blocked from preview and download and are retained with typed reason codes.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Prior quarantined versions remain addressable by immutable ref so false-positive review or manual release never erases history.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Security review or legal hold freezes delete and copy-promote actions.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: No automatic cold transition before review or release posture is settled.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Quarantine never yields customer-facing derivatives. Release creates a new clean object boundary instead.

lifecycle_rows.label: Restore archive
lifecycle_rows.current_version_posture: Restore bundles remain durable evidence for checkpoint and reopen verification.
lifecycle_rows.prior_version_posture: Earlier restore bundles remain independently addressable by checkpoint and candidate identity.
lifecycle_rows.hold_or_legal_block_posture: Restore evidence remains protected while release or incident review depends on it.
lifecycle_rows.transition_posture: Archive-tier storage is allowed when restore timing and evidence retrieval remain within the declared verification window.
lifecycle_rows.source_expiry_effect_on_derivatives: Derived restore reports may survive only with limitation notes if the underlying bundle or body ages out.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Artifact retention matrix explicit gaps and retention classes
source_refs.rationale: The corpus publishes retention classes but still lacks one canonical per-artifact duration matrix, so lifecycle rules must encode the gap explicitly.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Truth boundary statement and quarantine posture
source_refs.rationale: The object-store topology has to preserve the already-fixed quarantine, preview, and extraction boundary from upload intake safety work.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Release rows and history-preserving copy/promote semantics
source_refs.rationale: Quarantine release is evidence-bound and must preserve history rather than mutating a quarantined object into clean state in place.

notes: Retention classes and limitation behavior are fixed now even though exact age-based durations remain a published source gap.
Lifecycle transitions remain subordinate to legal hold, privacy reconciliation, and restore admissibility.
